select_when: A seemingly better alternative exists, but economic, psychological, or operational friction is keeping people on the incumbent path.
A migration or vendor exit plan is being described as reversible even though coexistence drag and reversal cost are quietly growing.
The team needs to distinguish real fit from loyalty driven by sunk effort, habit, or commitment-consistency pressure.
A recommendation must surface the hidden cost of dual-run dependency, exit sequencing pressure, or delayed switching.
avoid_when: The situation is genuinely novel enough that clinging to the incumbent frame would mainly reinforce cognitive entrenchment.
The model is being used to justify inertia without naming the concrete coordination, dependency, or reversal costs that make switching hard.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on incumbent friction, dual-run drag, and reversibility decay rather than widening it into generic loyalty or persuasion language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor migration-policy sprawl beyond the raw source's stronger coexistence, reversal-cost, and framing material.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains Wave 1 to Wave 3 only.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans platform exits, cognitive inertia, and communication design.

select_when.note: The source is explicit that switching costs matter most when friction, coexistence drag, and reversal-cost growth keep actors on an incumbent path.

avoid_when.note: The novelty warning is source-explicit; the second avoid condition is conservative normalization of the source's anti-entrenchment and dependency-mapping warnings.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on incumbent-friction transitions, while the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source yields reversibility and switching-friction analysis, but the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: The model works through counterfactual switching and system-friction effects, while the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
